title: Add model field to prompt files
description: Add `model:` frontmatter field to all 6 `.github/prompts/*.prompt.md` files to specify the preferred LLM model for each workflow. The `start` and `continue` prompts are compute-intensive (orchestration) and benefit from specifying `gpt-4o` or `claude-sonnet`. The `figma-to-code` prompt benefits from a vision-capable model. Adding `model:` alongside the existing `agent:` (from TASK-SYS-020) and `tools:` (from TASK-SYS-021) completes the full execution context triple required by the VS Code prompt file specification (March 2026).
acceptance_criteria: Given start.prompt.md has `model: gpt-4o` (or equivalent capable model) in frontmatter, when /start is invoked, then the specified model is used for CTO orchestration
Given figma-to-code.prompt.md has a vision-capable model specified, when invoked, then a model capable of processing visual context is selected
Given all 6 prompt files have `model:` field, when each prompt is invoked, then the model field is respected by VS Code agent routing
Given `model:` is added after `agent:` and before `tools:` in frontmatter, then frontmatter ordering follows VS Code documentation conventions
Given TASK-SYS-020 (agent:) and TASK-SYS-021 (tools:) are complete, when model: is added, then all three execution context fields are present in every prompt file

history.details: Ticket created from TODO/tasks/L2-vscode-copilot-improvements.md — gap identified in L2-06-D: model: frontmatter not covered by TASK-SYS-020 (agent:) or TASK-SYS-021 (tools:)
